title: Episode 19 Zeruel and Nervs situation
wikitext:
[]: Episode 19 - Zeruel and Nerv's situation             Quote Post by Leighton » Sat Mar 05, 2011 9:27 am Zeruel, being one of the most powerful angels yet, almost reached Adam not before nearly annihilating the nerve crew. Im not sure if I'm oblivious to anything obvious, but what would've Gendo done? Would he really have had to call Shinji for the first time? If Shinji was stubborn enough, I guess he would've let 3rd impact occur in the angels method.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Mar 05, 2011 1:16 pm We learn there's a self-destruct button somewhere on the base, so I guess they would've pulled the little red button.

[]:             Quote Post by Jessemon » Sat Mar 05, 2011 1:33 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: We learn there's a self-destruct button somewhere on the base, so I guess they would've pulled the little red button.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Mar 05, 2011 2:26 pm Yui is contractually required to save the day (or destroy the world) when the plot demands it. So if Shinji hadn't shown up, she probably would have eventually done something. But she had to give Gendo the finger a few times first.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Sat Mar 05, 2011 3:48 pm Well remember, Zeruel goes to kill the bridge crew before going to meet Adam. So if Shinji was no-where to be found, they probably would've dicked around with the dummy plug some more until Zeruel blew them all up and then went down to Terminal Dogma. Yui, powerful as she is, is still very limited without a pilot. If she didn't need one, why would Gendo need Shinji in the first place?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Mar 05, 2011 3:58 pm I guess by the same token, if Yui needed a pilot in order to prevent the sort of impending doom that might make all her years of planning a total waste, and Shinji wasn't necessarily going to show up, why reject the dummy system? Having a plug in there probably makes things easier, but it doesn't seem to be an absolute requirement for these testy older models. Remember how Eva-00 ejected Rei's plug and kept right on going?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Mar 05, 2011 5:48 pm I guess Yui would eventually have ceased her protests - she wants her husband to go and talk to their son, but she'd stop at the point where her protest would have resulted in the death of the two.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Sat Mar 05, 2011 6:24 pm Hmmm... perhaps Yui knew she wanted to munch on Zeruel for the S2 engine, and that if she were to accept the dummy plug; she'd be giving Gendo control over it. By waiting for Shinji and continuing to reject the plug, it's her way of telling him "I'm a God now, and if you try to tell me what to do - I'll bite your goddamn head off"

[]:             Quote Post by AshPhoenix » Sat Mar 05, 2011 6:52 pm But why would Yui wait for Zeruel? She could have easily eaten, say, Shamshel's engine, or even maybe Sachiel's. And I don't think her motives were as sincere as having some lovely father-son moments, either...I think that it's closer to what EvangelionFan, where both Yui and Gendo were trying to assert their authority (more so Gendo). Gendo probably just didn't want to face Shinji after watching him leave yet again.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Mar 05, 2011 7:01 pm AshPhoenix wrote: But why would Yui wait for Zeruel?

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Sat Mar 05, 2011 7:02 pm AshPhoenix wrote: But why would Yui wait for Zeruel? She could have easily eaten, say, Shamshel's engine, or even maybe Sachiel's.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Mar 05, 2011 7:16 pm EvangelionFan wrote: she would have needed Shinji or a dummy plug to go anywhere

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Sat Mar 05, 2011 7:20 pm Reichu wrote: Not necessarily true. We know that Yui can initiate some movement with even a signal termination plug in place; and if Eva-00 is any precedent, she might be able to go all out even with nothing in there at all.

[]:             Quote Post by AshPhoenix » Sat Mar 05, 2011 7:24 pm EvangelionFan wrote: At the least, I still think Yui would have needed Shinji to overpower Zeruel in the way that she did.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Mar 05, 2011 7:33 pm I used the case of Eva-00 as an indication that Evas can move of their own volition without having an entry plug inserted (i.e. no pilot to interface with). So Yui should be theoretically capable of achieving at least the same; and she can move without power in the absence of a pilot as well. The evidence seems pretty mixed as to exactly how having the pilot there benefits a berserk Eva, aside from maybe "making it easier" in some way. I don't really understand how Shinji's sync being maxed out to the point that he's goo would give Yui better access to her magical Eva powers, or whatever. (I'm not sure I ever really "got" the whole 400% sync thing in the first place...) Though I'm all ears. Er, eyes. A lot of Yui's timing seems to be dictated more by drama than anything else, but perhaps I'm just not being creative enough.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Sat Mar 05, 2011 7:42 pm AshPhoenix wrote: But why Shinji?

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Sat Mar 05, 2011 7:55 pm Also, the effects of being inside a giant bio-mechanical thing may have had an effect on Yui, specifically she might not be completely sane. Even if she is capable of fighting Zeruel on her own, she might irrationally stubborn enough that she'll let humanity die if she doesn't get to do things "her way." And while Unit 00 did move without a plug, it's berserker rage started when Rei was in the plug. So why an Eva may not need a pilot to continue berserk mode they may need a pilot to get them there. Besides Unit 00's movements were sort of jerky, not fluid and agile like an Eva would need to be in order to defeat Zeruel.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Mar 05, 2011 8:10 pm Reichu wrote: I don't really understand how Shinji's sync being maxed out to the point that he's goo would give Yui better access to her magical Eva powers, or whatever. (I'm not sure I ever really "got" the whole 400% sync thing in the first place...) Though I'm all ears. Er, eyes.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Mar 05, 2011 8:13 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: FWIW, this fits in perfectly on the allegorical/thematic level too.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Mar 05, 2011 8:38 pm Reichu wrote: Are you implying that my subconscious is really my mom?
